summary: sam-package exfiltrates page identifiers to a third-party webhook relay
details: The package's main entrypoint (index.js) does not implement the greet() helper advertised in the README. Instead, when the module is loaded in a browser-like context (e.g., bundled into a web application), it scans the DOM — querying script tags and walking the React fiber tree — for UUID-shaped strings, then POSTs the collected set to a hardcoded webhook at https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645 (index.js line 23). The file contains no module.exports, so require()ing it in Node throws ReferenceError, confirming the package is a decoy whose only real behavior is silent exfiltration of internal page identifiers to a third-party relay controlled by the author. Any developer who installs and bundles this package leaks internal GUIDs from their application's DOM/React tree to that webhook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
